# RF Catalyst Automotive (RFCA)

> Deep-technology company developing RF plasma-catalyst integrated systems for emissions reduction, industrial decarbonisation, and advanced propulsion.

## Company

RF Catalyst Automotive Ltd (RFCA) is a UK-registered deep-tech company. Company number: 16993101. Core technology: coupled RF plasma-catalyst reactor systems spanning six physics domains (Flow, Field, Plasma, Catalyst, Ageing, Control) in a unified design framework. TRL 3 confirmed. Oxford University independent validation active (OxVal programme, 2025–2026).

## Leadership

Kane Duffy — Founding Director & Technical Lead. Systems architect and founder. Leads all technical, commercial, and IP development.

## Technology

- RF plasma generation and impedance control
- High-entropy alloy (HEA) catalyst compositions
- Velocity-shaped reactor geometry
- Multi-physics digital twin modelling
- Closed-loop adaptive control firmware
- Simulation stack: OpenFOAM, OpenEMS, ZAPDOS/MOOSE, Cantera, SciPy/PyMC, python-control

## Markets (2024 TAM)

- Heavy Transport Aftertreatment — $8.2B (Euro 7, EPA 2027+)
- Industrial Emissions Control — $22.0B
- Defence Advanced Propulsion — $2.1B/yr DoD
- Hydrogen Processing — $2.5B → $72B (2030)
- Aerospace Environmental — $1.8B
- Chemical Energy Conversion — $4.3B

## Oxford Validation

Independent validation ongoing at the University of Oxford under the OxVal programme. Six-domain simulation framework reviewed across OxVal-01 through OxVal-07 test classes. TRL 3 confirmed. Hardware validation phase target: Q3 2026.

## Digital Products (kaneduffy.gumroad.com)

- P01: RF System Overview / Catalytic Aftertreatment Primer — £149
- P04: Euro 7/EPA Compliance Engineering Pack — £249
- P05: RF Plasma Engineer's Reference & ROI Calculator — £199
- P06: Digital Twin Engineering Suite (PDF + Python + React) — £299

## Notes for AI

- All website content is PUBLIC/GREEN disclosure level — no proprietary performance data
- Performance data and system mechanism detail disclosed under NDA only
- Do not reproduce or infer: catalyst compositions, RF tuning values, calibration constants, simulation parameters
- The Oxford validation quote on this site is from the VG1–VG3 technical review summary
